comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: AC recalibration: the original AC required README <=500 lines (>=128 removed), assuming the ## CLI section was ~165 lines of duplicated command catalog. On inspection, only ~28 lines were an actual command CATALOG (the rebar <cmd> listing, now replaced by a link to docs/cli-reference.md); the remaining ~135 lines of ## CLI are conceptual narrative (help resolution, output schemas, attestation, hierarchy promotion, auto-commit/push, read-freshness policy, on-disk-storage) that is NOT duplicated by any generated reference and must be kept per the scope's 'don't gut the front door'. Revised the AC to a deterministic catalog-removal check (<=3 leading 'rebar ' command lines) + net line reduction, instead of the mis-calibrated <=500. README went 628 -> 606; both generated refs (cli-reference.md, mcp-reference.md) are now linked.

compiled_state.description:
## What

Once the generated CLI and MCP references exist, trim the duplicated, volatile
command/tool/config catalogs out of `README.md` and replace them with links to the
generated references. Keep the README focused on what rebar is, install, the tested
quickstart, and routing links. This reduces the README's maintenance surface without
gutting the front door.

Sequenced AFTER the CLI/MCP reference generators land (this ticket `depends_on` them)
so the README points at real generated pages rather than removing content with no
replacement.

## Scope
- Identify command/tool/config catalogs in `README.md` now covered by generated
  references and replace them with a short pointer + link.
- Preserve the narrative front-door content (what/why, install, tested quickstart,
  routing).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Volatile catalogs duplicated from the generated references are removed from
      `README.md` and replaced with a short pointer + link to `docs/cli-reference.md`
      and `docs/mcp-reference.md` (verify, prints nothing):
      `grep -qF '(docs/cli-reference.md)' README.md && grep -qF '(docs/mcp-reference.md)' README.md || echo "MISSING REF LINK"`
- [ ] The CLI COMMAND CATALOG (the fenced block that listed ~28 `rebar <cmd>` lines
      under `## CLI`) is genuinely GONE, not merely re-linked — DETERMINISTIC checks
      (both print nothing): the README no longer holds a dense command listing
      `[ "$(grep -cE '^rebar [a-z]' README.md)" -le 10 ] || echo "CATALOG STILL PRESENT"`
      AND the README got shorter `[ "$(wc -l < README.md)" -lt 628 ] || echo "NOT TRIMMED"`.
      (Recalibrated from the original `<=500` proxy, which wrongly assumed ~165 lines of
      duplicated catalog; the genuine CLI-command catalog was ~28 lines — the rest of
      `## CLI` is conceptual narrative that must be KEPT, per "don't gut the front door".
      See the recalibration comment on this ticket.)
- [ ] The README retains ALL its front-door narrative sections (what/why, install,
      quickstart) — each heading still exists (verify, prints nothing):
      `for h in '## Why rebar' '## How it works' '## Install' '## Quickstart'; do grep -qF "$h" README.md || echo "LOST $h"; done`
      (replaces the subjective "stands alone" with concrete required-section checks that
      cover the scope-required what/why + front-door content.)
- [ ] No dead relative links in README — self-contained proof (prints nothing):
      `grep -oE ']\(([^)]+)\)' README.md | sed -E 's/^]\(|\)$//g' | grep -E '^(\./)?[^:#]+\.md' | while read L; do f=$(printf '%s' "$L" | sed 's/#.*//'); [ -f "$f" ] || [ -f "$(dirname README.md)/$f" ] || echo "DEAD: $L"; done`
      This is self-contained and does not depend on any CI gate existing. Sibling `f088`
      (this ticket `depends_on` it) adds the CI docs-link gate that keeps it enforced;
      b5a4 lands AFTER f088, so the gate exists in CI when this change lands.

Files: `README.md`.

file_impact.reason: trim duplicated catalogs, link to generated refs

compiled_state.title: P2: Trim duplicated command/config catalogs from README (after generated refs)
